ddls:
CREATE TABLE "measurement_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the measurement type (e.g., Nominal, Ordinal, Interval, Ratio) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description explaining the characteristics of the measurement type */,
  "zero_treatment" TEXT /* Description of how zero is treated in this measurement type */,
  "example" TEXT /* Example illustrating the measurement type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_samples" (
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data sample */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the measurement type of the sample */,
  "sample_value" TEXT /* Value of the data sample */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the sample was added */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the user who added the sample */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_samples_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_samples_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES measurement_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "zero_treatment" (
  "treatment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each zero treatment rule */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the measurement type related to the zero treatment */,
  "zero_description" TEXT /* Description of how zero is interpreted in this context */,
  "example" TEXT /* Example illustrating the zero treatment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("treatment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_zero_treatment_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES measurement_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_explanations" (
  "explanation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each explanation */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the measurement type */,
  "explanation_text" TEXT /* Detailed explanation of the measurement type's implications */,
  "example" TEXT /* Example illustrating the explanation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("explanation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_explanations_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES measurement_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the user role (e.g., Admin, Analyst, Viewer) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user account */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user's role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_visualizations" (
  "visualization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each visualization */,
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data sample */,
  "visualization_type" TEXT /* Type of visualization (e.g., bar chart, line chart, etc.) */,
  "configuration" TEXT /* Configuration of the visualization (e.g., colors, labels, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visualization_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_visualizations_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_id") REFERENCES data_samples ("sample_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "visualization_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data visualization */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_content" TEXT /* Content of the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_visualization_id FOREIGN KEY ("visualization_id") REFERENCES data_visualizations ("visualization_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who accessed the data */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the data was accessed */,
  "accessed_table" TEXT /* Name of the table that was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
